text.title: success-metrics-definition Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for success-metrics-definition as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: North Star chosen for measurability rather than truth drives wrong behavior. Example: page views instead of successful task completions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This is Goodhart's law in action: any metric that becomes a target stops being a good metric. Always define guardrails.

failure-mode.description: AARRR is funnel-shaped, bad fit for cyclical or habit products. Consider HEART, RICE, or ICE for those.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Not all products follow the acquisition to referral funnel. Choose a framework that matches your product lifecycle.

failure-mode.description: Targets set without a baseline are theatrical. Agents will pick round numbers like 10 or 20 percent that aren't grounded.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Targets without baselines have no credibility and lead to sandbagging or gaming.

failure-mode.description: B2B and marketplace metrics need segmentation by tier or cohort. A single MAU number hides churn.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Aggregated metrics hide crucial variation across segments and can mask real problems.
